description: Legal record from the early thirteenth century explicating a communal conflict. The sermon of local Jewish judge describes accusations concerning women in the city lending money with interest, women being adorned and sitting bedecked in the marketplace, and people listening to musical instruments while drinking. (S. D. Goitein, Mediterranean Society, 1:256, 464; 5:42, 517; Oded Zinger, “What Sort of Sermon is this?” in Jews, Christians, and Muslims in Medieval and Early Modern Times: A festschrift in honor of Mark R. Cohen, 83-98) EMS
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בבית המדרש לרבנים.
